quote: Once an infidel and libertine, a servant of slaves in Africa, was, by the rich mercy of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ, preserved, restored, pardoned, and appointed to preach the faith he had long laboured to destroy.
source: Self-composed epitaph, his tomb at Olney parish church

quote: The practice of truth is the most profitable reading of it.
source: Morning and Evening (1866), June 20 entry

quote: Soul-winning is the chief business of the Christian minister.
source: The Soul-Winner (1895)

quote: Every generation needs regeneration.
source: The Salt-Cellars (1885), Vol. 1

quote: Care more for a grain of faith than a ton of excitement.
source: Lectures to My Students (1870)

quote: So I awoke, and behold it was a dream.
source: The Pilgrim's Progress (1678), closing line

quote: He that is down needs fear no fall, he that is low no pride.
source: The Pilgrim's Progress, Part II, Ch. VI (Shepherd Boy's Song)

quote: God Almighty has set before me two great objects: the suppression of the Slave Trade and the reformation of manners.
source: Diary entry, 28 October 1787

quote: Let us not despair; it is a blessed cause, and success, ere long, will crown our exertions.
source: Speech to the House of Commons, 18 April 1791

quote: Thank God that I should have lived to witness a day in which England is willing to give twenty millions sterling for the abolition of slavery.
source: House of Commons, 26 July 1833

quote: I believe in Christianity as I believe that the sun has risen, not only because I see it, but because by it I see everything else.
source: "Is Theology Poetry?" (1945)

quote: God whispers to us in our pleasures, speaks in our conscience, but shouts in our pains: it is His megaphone to rouse a deaf world.
source: The Problem of Pain (1940)

quote: Either this man was, and is, the Son of God: or else a madman or something worse.
source: Mere Christianity (1952), Book II, Ch. 3

quote: Aim at heaven and you will get earth thrown in; aim at earth and you get neither.
source: Mere Christianity (1952), Book III, Ch. 10

quote: It is Christ Himself, not the Bible, who is the true Word of God.
source: Letter, 8 November 1952; published in Letters of C. S. Lewis (1966)

quote: All God's giants have been weak men, who did great things for God because they reckoned on His being with them.
source: Quoted in Hudson Taylor and the China Inland Mission (1918)

quote: A little thing is a little thing, but faithfulness in little things is a great thing.
source: Hudson Taylor and China's Open Century, Book Four

quote: There was a day when I died, utterly died, died to George Muller, his opinions, preferences, tastes, and will; died to the world, its approval or censure; died to the approval or blame even of my brethren and friends.
source: Quoted in George Muller of Bristol: His Life of Prayer and Faith by A. T. Pierson (1899)

quote: Prayer and faith, the universal remedies against every want and every difficulty.
source: A Narrative of Some of the Lord's Dealings with George Muller, Written by Himself, Third Part

quote: Faith is a living, daring confidence in God's grace, so sure and certain that the believer would stake his life on it a thousand times.
source: Preface to the Epistle of St. Paul to the Romans (1522)

quote: Be a sinner and sin boldly, but believe and rejoice in Christ even more boldly, for he is victorious over sin, death, and the world.
source: Letter to Philip Melanchthon, 1 August 1521; Luther's Works, Vol. 48, pp. 281-282

quote: I look on all the world as my parish.
source: Journal, 11 June 1739

quote: Though I am always in haste, I am never in a hurry.
source: Letter, 10 December 1777

quote: An ounce of love is worth a pound of knowledge.
source: Letter to Joseph Benson, 7 November 1768

quote: Resolved, never to do anything which I should be afraid to do if it were the last hour of my life.
source: The Resolutions, No. 7 (1722-1723)

quote: Resolved, never to lose one moment of time, but improve it the most profitable way I possibly can.
source: The Resolutions, No. 5 (1722-1723)

quote: The God that holds you over the pit of hell, much as one holds a spider or some loathsome insect over the fire, abhors you, and is dreadfully provoked.
source: Sinners in the Hands of an Angry God (1741)

quote: Forgiveness is an act of the will, and the will can function regardless of the temperature of the heart.
source: Tramp for the Lord (1974)

quote: It is not on our forgiveness any more than on our goodness that the world's healing hinges, but on His.
source: The Hiding Place (1971)

quote: Cheap grace is the deadly enemy of our Church.
source: The Cost of Discipleship (1937)

quote: Costly grace is the gospel which must be sought again and again.
source: The Cost of Discipleship (1937)

quote: When Christ calls a man, he bids him come and die.
source: The Cost of Discipleship (1937)

quote: If I have not compassion on my fellow-servant even as my Lord had pity on me, then I know nothing of Calvary love.

If I say, "Yes, I forgive, but I cannot forget," as though the God who twice a day washes all the sands on all the shores of all the world, could not wash such memories from my mind, then I know nothing of Calvary love.
In acceptance lieth peace.
source: Poem, "In Acceptance Lieth Peace" (1915)

quote: What comes into our minds when we think about God is the most important thing about us.
source: The Knowledge of the Holy (1961)

quote: It is doubtful whether God can bless a man greatly until He has hurt him deeply.
source: The Root of the Righteous (1955), Ch. 39

quote: Let a man set his heart only on doing the will of God and he is instantly free.
source: The Pursuit of God (1948)

quote: Worship is the missing jewel of the evangelical church.
source: Worship: The Missing Jewel, compiled in Whatever Happened to Worship? ed. Gerald B. Smith (1985)

quote: Life is short, and every day is a gift from God.
source: Just As I Am: The Autobiography of Billy Graham (1997)

quote: When a brave man takes a stand, the spines of others are often stiffened.
source: Reader's Digest, July 1964

quote: If perfect earthly sight were offered me to-morrow, I would not accept it.
source: Fanny Crosby's Life-Story (1903)

quote: Once a day, especially in the early years of life and study, call yourselves to an account what new ideas, what new proposition or truth you have gained.
source: The Improvement of the Mind (1727)

quote: Do not hover always on the surface of things, nor take up suddenly with mere appearances, but penetrate into the depth of matters.
source: The Improvement of the Mind (1727)

quote: I believe no man was ever scolded out of his sins.
source: Letter to John Newton, 17 June 1783

quote: Without knowledge of self there is no knowledge of God.
source: Institutes of the Christian Religion, Book 1, Ch. 1

quote: It is better to limp in the way, than run with the greatest swiftness out of it.
source: Institutes of the Christian Religion, Book 1, Ch. 6

quote: There is not one little blade of grass, there is no color in this world, that is not intended to make men rejoice.
source: Sermon on I Corinthians

quote: Expect great things from God; attempt great things for God.
source: Attributed to Carey's 1792 sermon at Nottingham (the "Deathless Sermon"); earliest print citations appear in missionary periodicals of the 1840s

quote: I am immortal till my work is accomplished.
source: Private journal entry, 1853 (wording varies slightly across published editions of his journals)

quote: Go for souls and go for the worst.
source: Quoted in The Life of General Booth (1913) by Hulda Friedrichs

quote: My business is to get the world saved.
source: "The Risks," The War Cry, 20 December 1884 (excerpted from a longer sentence)

quote: Character is what you are in the dark.
source: Attributed by his son, William R. Moody, in his biography of Moody

quote: Oh Lord! You've been wid me in six troubles, don't desert me in the seventh!
source: Harriet, the Moses of Her People (1886) by Sarah Hopkins Bradford

quote: This hand hath offended.
source: Reported by eyewitnesses at his execution, Oxford, 21 March 1556

quote: The woman was made of a rib out of the side of Adam; not made out of his head to rule over him, nor out of his feet to be trampled upon by him, but out of his side to be equal with him, under his arm to be protected, and near his heart to be beloved.
source: Commentary on the Whole Bible, Genesis 2:21

quote: Whatever we have, the property of it is God's; we have only the use of it, according to the direction of our great Lord, and for his honour.
source: Commentary on the Whole Bible, Luke 16

quote: I bless the Lord that he gave me counsel.
source: Reported last words, 1661

quote: I hang by a thread, but it is, if I may so speak, of Christ's spinning.
source: Letter 56, to Lady Kenmure

quote: I see grace groweth best in winter.
source: Letter 74, to Lady Culross

quote: Certainty is the mark of the common-sense life: gracious uncertainty is the mark of the spiritual life.
source: My Utmost for His Highest (1956), April 30 entry

quote: Let the past sleep, but let it sleep on the bosom of Christ.
source: My Utmost for His Highest (1956), December 31 entry

quote: The direction in which science will move is set by the philosophic world view of the scientists.
source: How Should We Then Live? (1976), Ch. 1

quote: If society gives the rights, it can take them away, they're not inalienable.
source: A Christian Manifesto (1982)

quote: There can be no mistake more inexcusable and fatal than to doubt, disobey, or neglect the Bible.
source: The Divine Origin of the Bible (1899)

quote: He is cunning, he is mighty, he never rests, he is ever plotting the downfall of the child of God; and if the child of God relaxes in prayer the Devil will succeed in ensnaring him.
source: Power and Peace in Prayer (1924)

quote: Oh, no single piece of our mental world is to be hermetically sealed off from the rest, and there is not a square inch in the whole domain of our human existence over which Christ, who is Sovereign over all, does not cry: "Mine!"
source: Sphere Sovereignty (1880), p. 488; cited in Abraham Kuyper: A Centennial Reader, ed. James D. Bratt (1998)

quote: Salvation in Christ to the very uttermost, but out of Christ no salvation at all.
source: Only One Way (1850)

quote: Ignorance of Scripture is the root of every error in religion.
source: Expository Thoughts on the Gospels: St. John (1865-1873)

quote: Prayer will consume sin, or sin will choke prayer.
source: A Call to Prayer (1867)

quote: Now let me burn out for God.
source: Journal and Letters of the Rev. Henry Martyn (1837), p. 447

quote: Going to church on Sunday does not make you a Christian any more than going into a garage makes you an automobile!
source: Quoted in Press, Radio, Television, Periodicals, Public Relations, and Advertising (1967), ed. John Eldridge Drewry

quote: I have no interest in a God who does not smite.
source: Quoted in Billy Sunday Was His Real Name (1955), University of Chicago Press, p. 179

quote: To live coram Deo is to live one's entire life in the presence of God, under the authority of God, to the glory of God.
source: In the Presence of God: Devotional Readings on the Attributes of God (2003)

quote: I'm so thankful for the active obedience of Christ; no hope without it.
source: Telegram to Prof. John Murray, 1 January 1937 (his last words)

quote: Wherever you are, be all there.
source: Journal entry, quoted in Shadow of the Almighty by Elisabeth Elliot (1958), p. 47

quote: Suffering is never for nothing.
source: Suffering Is Never for Nothing (2019)

quote: He has chosen not to heal me, but to hold me.
source: A Place of Healing: Wrestling with the Mysteries of Suffering, Pain, and God's Sovereignty (2010)

quote: A faith that can be destroyed by suffering is not faith.
source: If Prison Walls Could Speak (1972)

quote: It was in prison that we found the hope of salvation for the Communists.
source: Tortured for Christ (1967), p. 58

quote: If God spare my life, ere many years I will cause a boy that driveth the plough to know more of the Scripture than thou dost.
source: Reported response to a clergyman, quoted in Foxe's Book of Martyrs

quote: Truth prevails above all.
source: Letter to Christian of Prachatice; later adopted as the Hussite motto ("Veritas vincit")

quote: O sancta simplicitas!
source: Reportedly spoken while burning at the stake, upon seeing an elderly peasant add wood to his pyre, 6 July 1415

quote: It is not what we do that matters, but what a sovereign God chooses to do through us.
source: Loving God (1997)

quote: If you hate somebody, you're not hurting the person you hate, you're hurting yourself.
source: Devil at My Heels: A Heroic Olympian's Astonishing Story of Survival as a Japanese POW in World War II

quote: Only one life, 'twill soon be past, only what's done for Christ will last.
source: From his poem "Only One Life, 'Twill Soon Be Past"

quote: Humility is the displacement of self by the enthronement of God.
source: Humility (1895)

quote: Humility, the place of entire dependence on God, is the first duty and the highest virtue of the creature, and the root of every virtue.
source: Humility (1895)

quote: Why should we not... do something that will prove that we are really in earnest in claiming to be followers of him who, though he was rich, for our sake became poor?
source: Letter to the Foreign Mission Journal, 15 September 1887, Tungchow, China

quote: Mr. Dickson, you are advanced in years now, and your own prospect is soon to be laid in the grave, there to be eaten by worms; I confess to you, that if I can but live and die serving and honoring the Lord Jesus, it will make no difference to me whether I am eaten by Cannibals or by worms; and in the Great Day my resurrection body will arise as fair as yours in the likeness of our risen Redeemer.
source: John G. Paton, Missionary to the New Hebrides: An Autobiography (1889)

quote: I believe that in the end the truth will conquer.
source: Statement to the Duke of Lancaster, 1381

quote: Whatever weakens your reason, impairs the tenderness of your conscience, obscures your sense of God, takes off your relish for spiritual things, whatever increases the authority of the body over the mind, that thing is sin to you, however innocent it may seem in itself.
source: Letter to John Wesley, 8 June 1725

quote: I am prepared to die in the army of Jesus.
source: Spoken as a vow at his conversion, 1948; quoted in Janani: The Making of a Martyr by Margaret Ford (1978)

quote: One word became unbelievably clear, and that word was privilege.
source: Address at Urbana Missionary Conference, 1976

quote: I may not like Idi Amin but if I am a Christian believer I am obliged to forgive him, love him with Christ's love, and want the highest and best for his life.
source: Press conference remark on the title of I Love Idi Amin (1977)

quote: The bigger the darkness, the easier it is to spot your little light.
source: God's Smuggler (1967)

quote: People are never the enemy, only the devil.
source: God's Smuggler (1967)

quote: Love is positive, is eternal: violence is degeneracy, it is its own destruction.
source: The Religion of Jesus (1931 ed.)

quote: God, if You want me to, I will spend the rest of my life in India trying to help these women.
source: Her recorded prayer, 1898, following the deaths of three women in one night; widely documented in biographical accounts of her decision to become a medical missionary

quote: I desire no higher enjoyment in this life, than to be instrumental of leading some poor, ignorant heathen females, to the knowledge of the Savior.
source: Memoir of Mrs. Ann H. Judson, ed. James D. Knowles (1830)

quote: We shall this day light such a candle, by God's grace, in England, as I trust shall never be put out.
source: Said to Nicholas Ridley at their execution, Oxford, 16 October 1555; recorded in Foxe's Book of Martyrs (1563)

quote: True faith or true knowledge begets love, and love begets obedience to the commandments of God.
source: The Complete Works of Menno Simons (1871 ed.), p. 246

quote: And now from the depths of my heart, I too prayed for God to forgive my torturers, and I determined by the aid of Christ to do my best to acquaint the Japanese people with the message of salvation that they might become as other believing Christians.
source: "I Was a Prisoner of Japan," as told to Don R. Falkenberg, The Bible Meditation League (1950)

quote: Talk to Him as if He were your father.
source: I Dared to Call Him Father (1978)

quote: Our object is the improvement of the spiritual condition of the young men engaged in houses of business, by the formation of Bible classes, family and social prayer meetings, mutual improvement societies, or any other spiritual agency.
source: Founding statement of the YMCA, 6 June 1844

quote: A Day Prayer Meeting is held every Wednesday from 12 to 1 o'clock in the Consistory building of the North Dutch Church, corner of Fulton and William Streets.
source: Handbill announcing the prayer meeting that sparked the 1857 Prayer Revival

quote: I had a strong conviction that the ability to write Living Letters was a special gift from God.
source: Quoted in Today's Christian Living and God Reports

quote: We must have something of substance to say in our worship [services] that reminds us why Christ's story is so unique and so utterly essential.
source: Interview with John W. Kennedy, Beliefnet, 5 December 2014

quote: My earnest desire is to re-model the whole plan of missionary operations so as to bring them to the simple standard of God's word.
source: Christian Devotedness (1834), p. 214

quote: To put it bluntly and plainly, if Christ is not my Substitute, I still occupy the place of a condemned sinner.
source: The Apostolic Preaching of the Cross (1955), p. 410
